# Cron Scheduling Guide

Guide to scheduling agents using cron expressions with tokio-cron-scheduler.

## Overview

Switchboard uses 5-field cron expressions to schedule agent executions. The scheduler runs in the background and triggers agent tasks based on their configured schedules.

## Cron Expression Format

```
┌───────────── minute (0-59)
│ ┌───────────── hour (0-23)
│ │ ┌───────────── day of month (1-31)
│ │ │ ┌───────────── month (1-12)
│ │ │ │ ┌───────────── day of week (0-6, 0=Sunday)
│ │ │ │ │
* * * * *
```

## Special Characters

| Character | Meaning | Example |
|-----------|---------|---------|
| `*` | Any value | `* * * * *` = every minute |
| `*/n` | Every n units | `*/15 * * * *` = every 15 minutes |
| `,` | List values | `0 9 * * 1,3,5` = Mon/Wed/Fri at 9 AM |
| `-` | Range | `0 9 * * 1-5` = weekdays at 9 AM |

## Configuration

Agents are configured in your `switchboard.toml` file:

```toml
[[agent]]
name = "daily-report"
schedule = "0 9 * * *"  # Daily at 9:00 AM
prompt = "Generate a daily summary of codebase changes."
```

### Required Fields

- `name` - Unique agent identifier
- `schedule` - Cron expression
- `prompt` OR `prompt_file` - Task definition

## Timezone Support

Switchboard respects the timezone setting in your configuration:

```toml
[settings]
timezone = "America/New_York"  # IANA timezone format
```

The timezone can also be set to `"system"` to use the local system timezone.

## Common Examples

| Expression | Description |
|------------|-------------|
| `* * * * *` | Every minute |
| `*/5 * * * *` | Every 5 minutes |
| `*/15 * * * *` | Every 15 minutes |
| `0 * * * *` | Every hour at :00 |
| `0 9 * * *` | Daily at 9:00 AM |
| `0 9 * * 1-5` | Weekdays at 9:00 AM |
| `0 8 * * 1` | Every Monday at 8:00 AM |
| `0 0 1 * *` | First day of every month |
| `0 9,17 * * *` | Twice daily at 9 AM and 5 PM |

> **Tip:** Use [crontab.guru](https://crontab.guru) to build and verify cron expressions.

## Troubleshooting

### Agent not running at expected time

1. Check timezone setting in configuration
2. Verify cron expression is valid
3. Ensure scheduler is running: `switchboard status`
4. Check logs: `switchboard logs`

### Skipped executions

When overlap_mode is set to "skip", executions may be skipped if a previous run is still in progress. See [Configuration](configuration.md#overlap-modes) for details.
